checks.details: All documented claims verified against code. POST /api/chat/stream exists at src/app/api/chat/stream/route.ts and returns Content-Type: text/event-stream (SSE). Admin auth reads the 'x-api-key' header (confirmed in src/lib/utils/auth.ts:25) and compares against ADMIN_API_KEY from env. ADMIN_API_KEY is defined in src/lib/config/env.ts as optional. The POST /api/index body format '{ repoId: "owner/repo" }' matches the indexInputSchema in src/lib/utils/validation.ts. The file path src/lib/github/config.ts and the DEFAULT_REPOS export name are correct. All documented npm scripts and access URLs are valid. The .env.example ADMIN_API_KEY placeholder and comment accurately describe its purpose and header name.

Terminology is consistent across README and .env.example: both use 'ADMIN_API_KEY' for the variable name, 'x-api-key' for the header, and '/api/repos, /api/index' for the protected endpoints. Formatting in README is consistent (table columns, code fences, heading levels). The .env.example section heading '── Admin ──' follows the same style as all other sections. No contradictions found between the two updated files or between them and the existing docs.

All new public APIs are documented: POST /api/chat/stream is added to the API reference table with method, endpoint, description, and auth columns. ADMIN_API_KEY is documented in both the Setup section (step 3 callout) and in .env.example with a usage comment. The auth column was added to all rows in the API reference table, making auth requirements explicit for every endpoint. SSE event types (start, token, tool_call, done, error) are documented in the route source code; the README API reference describes the response format at an appropriate level of detail for end users. No public API, config option, or feature was found in the code that is missing from the docs.

summary: Documentation changes from Task 01 are accurate, consistent, and complete. The two updated files (README.md and .env.example) correctly document the streaming chat endpoint and admin authentication requirements. All claims were verified against the actual implementation. No issues were found and no fixes were required.
